mission: Define the tenant-isolation and ActingScopeTuple authority model so runtime isolation, audience visibility, purpose-of-use, organisation context, elevation, break-glass posture, and blast radius stay bound to one machine-checkable scope contract.

assumptions.statement: The tuple and blast-radius counts in this Phase 0 contract are declared authority counts for route classes and sample tuples; runtime issuance may refresh the exact live count later, but it may not omit or infer the fields.
Assistive sidecar work inherits the owner shell tuple and may not widen tenant, organisation, or purpose scope through a local selector or adjunct-only token.

defects.summary: Ambient role strings, remembered organisation selectors, and browser-local tenant state cannot substitute for one current ActingScopeTuple.
Runtime tenant isolation and browser route authority are explicitly joined through one runtime-browser authority binding.
Multi-tenant and platform work can no longer imply blast radius from route names or cohorts; affected tenant and organisation counts are first-class fields.
Governance scope tokens now derive from the same tuple and hash as cross-organisation support, hub, and release work.

scopeProfileCatalog.rationale: Assistive surfaces inherit the owner shell tuple and may never mint a shadow scope.
Platform governance binds one scope token and one tuple so release and access work cannot stay writable under stale scope.
Hub work is lawful only through an explicit declared organisation subset and one tuple hash.
Operations watch is broad by design but must surface explicit tenant and organisation counts before any control settles.
Patient self-service remains subject-bound and does not use a staff ActingScopeTuple.
Embedded patient routes preserve subject scope and degrade through channel-specific recovery instead of widening authority.
Secure-link recovery is narrower than a normal patient shell and cannot widen to staff scope.
Public intake remains pre-tenant and may not acquire staff authority semantics.
Servicing-site work keeps provider and dispatch scope separate from general staff routing.
Clinical and practice operations work must bind one current tuple and freeze on any scope drift.
Support-assisted capture can help inside a ticket scope but may not inherit replay or governance authority.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
scopeProfileCatalog.rationale: Support replay binds one selected anchor, evidence envelope, and tuple; restore freezes immediately on drift.
Support action work remains tuple-bound even when operating against a single ticket and tenant.

tenantIsolationModes.browserIsolationLaw: Browser surfaces remain lawful only through the published runtime binding and current route-to-scope requirement row for this isolation mode.
tenantIsolationModes.runtimeIsolationLaw: Gateway surface, trust-zone boundaries, runtime binding, and tenant-isolation mode must agree; no shell-local selector may widen them.
tenantIsolationModes.driftFreezeLaw: Any change to organisation scope, tenant scope, purpose, policy plane, visibility, elevation, or break-glass posture supersedes the current tuple and freezes mutation in place.
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 114 - tenant and acting context drift

blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay

actingScopeTuples.rationale: Clinical workspace routes bind one current organisation-scoped tuple and freeze on any scope or visibility drift.
Practice operations uses the same single-org scope law but with a distinct acting role and tuple hash.
Standard support ticket work uses one current tenant-scoped tuple and may not widen into replay or governance scope.
Support-assisted capture reuses the support surface but stays purpose-bound and tuple-distinct from replay or governance work.

source_refs: blueprint/phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#9C. Audit explorer, break-glass review, and support replay
actingScopeTuples.rationale: Support replay restore must keep selected-anchor scope, evidence admissibility, and break-glass posture inside one exact tuple.
Hub coordination uses an explicit organisation-group tuple so cross-organisation visibility and mutation freeze together.
Servicing-site work binds one current tuple and may not widen through queue, support, or return-path convenience.
Operations watch and intervention work remains broad-scope and must surface explicit blast radius before control settles.
Governance and release control derive from one current tuple and one governance scope token with explicit blast radius.

governanceScopeTokenBindings.rationale: Governance scope tokens are derived from the same ActingScopeTuple hash so governance scope cannot drift independently from live route authority.

runtimeBrowserAuthorityBindings.browserIsolationLaw: Browser route authority is valid only while the current AudienceSurfaceRuntimeBinding and published gateway surface set remain exact.
runtimeBrowserAuthorityBindings.runtimeIsolationLaw: Runtime topology, trust-zone crossings, gateway exposure, and tenant isolation stay bound to the same binding generation and may not be inferred separately.
